mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.5% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.5% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) above trend signals strengthening labor demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month growth and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or correlation reverses sign.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand, which compresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reduce equity exposure.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month simultaneously for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of dual-decline triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 1% month-over-month indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices near-term earnings upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly growth and 5-day SPX returns is below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or signal produces negative returns in more than 58% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 2.5%) signals monetary tightening that increases real discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.0% AND FEDFUNDS > 2.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of dual-condition triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.0% and remains elevated for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) growth above 1200 thousand units annualized signals strong residential demand and forward economic activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through multiplier effects on construction, materials, and consumer confidence.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST levels and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or correlation becomes negative.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contraction combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE > 3.8%) signals stagflationary pressure and supply-side weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as profit margins compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth falls below -0.5% AND UNRATE > 3.8%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of dual-condition triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
